description: Breadth across something is 'losha': the span between a river's banks or the clear width of a route. It can also widen the reach of an attitude, as 'losha ruesha' (wide compassion) does. 'whalo' (large) considers overall size, while 'lonai' (width) names the dimension itself.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and move into rounded mid-back 'o'. Raise the tongue blade toward the postalveolar region for voiceless 'sh', then open the mouth into central 'a'. Stress falls on 'lo'.
sound_symbolism: The mouth moves from rounded 'o' to open 'a' while 'sh' sustains the airflow between them. The gesture widens in the mouth, much as breadth does across a route.
usage_notes: 'losha luphore' gives a wide river and 'losha shoeka' a wide route. Figurative use describes breadth of reach rather than the size of an object.

semantic_domains.physical: large width across an opening or a span of land or water
semantic_domains.spatial: breadth measured from one side to the other
